title: Replace manual frequency stats with leakage-safe ordered categorical learning
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality categories (`Driver`, `Race`, combinations across years/regimes).
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality categories (`Driver`, `Race`, combinations across years/regimes). Current nodes mostly use raw frequencies and full-fold grouped medians, which can leak future information inside the fold and underuse rich category interactions. CatBoost’s ordered statistics were designed specifically to avoid prediction shift from target-based categorical processing.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost model using raw categorical columns plus a few explicit combos like `Race×Compound`, `Driver×Compound`, `Race×Stint`, with ordered boosting enabled and folds grouped by race chronology. Compare against the current manually encoded GBDT baseline; if helpful, use CatBoost only as a diversity model for stacking rather than as a full replacement.
expected_effect: Can outperform hand-built counts on rare drivers/races and reduce subtle within-fold leakage from full-sample category aggregates.
risk: If CV is not grouped causally, CatBoost can still look better locally than it generalizes; keep evaluation race-aware.
